focus: [D3-A11/D3] default-to-interface as a silent miss sink -- separate routed from routed-correctly; measure false-default rate; ambiguous->ask vs silent default

graphEvents.label: How should sk-design distinguish routed from routed-correctly when generic prompts silently default to interface?
The sk-design hub defaults generic design prompts to interface, but has no typed routeOutcome, defaultApplied, askRequired, or deferReason contract
mode-registry has enough alias corpus to measure false defaults, but stores no ambiguity/default policy fields
The skill-benchmark funnel gates intent/resource routing while modePrecision remains advisory, so wrong workflowMode or missing ask/defer is not a hard failure
The live parser exposes statedRoutingCorrect but currently leaves it null and does not parse workflowMode/defaultApplied/routeOutcome
A local alias proxy found a strict false-default rate of 29/46 (0.63) and a generous false-default rate of 4/46 (0.087), proving the metric is sensitive to untyped prose policy
Resource recall, context manifests, proof cards, and READY claims may count only after a parent-hub route is both present and correct against private gold
When private gold expects defer/ask for ambiguity, a silent interface default is a false default even if interface resources load

label: How should sk-design distinguish routed from routed-correctly when generic prompts silently default to interface?
The sk-design hub defaults generic design prompts to interface, but has no typed routeOutcome, defaultApplied, askRequired, or deferReason contract
mode-registry has enough alias corpus to measure false defaults, but stores no ambiguity/default policy fields

The live parser exposes statedRoutingCorrect but currently leaves it null and does not parse workflowMode/defaultApplied/routeOutcome
A local alias proxy found a strict false-default rate of 29/46 (0.63) and a generous false-default rate of 4/46 (0.087), proving the metric is sensitive to untyped prose policy
Resource recall, context manifests, proof cards, and READY claims may count only after a parent-hub route is both present and correct against private gold
When private gold expects defer/ask for ambiguity, a silent interface default is a false default even if interface resources load
Strict hub-prose classifier over registry aliases: 29 false interface defaults out of 46 non-interface aliases
Generous registry-aware classifier over registry aliases: 4 false interface defaults out of 46 non-interface aliases

direction: Treat any non-null route or loaded resource set as successful parent-hub routing
reason: This hides wrong workflowMode and ambiguous prompts that should ask/defer behind the interface default
